redacted: The live 'Account:' row carried the recording operator's ChatGPT login. It is replaced byte-for-byte with 'user@example.com' (16 chars in, 16 chars out), so every column of the box renders exactly as captured. This is the ONLY edit to bytes.raw.
notes: Hand-recorded /status box in PLAN collaboration mode — the required Plan fixture. Launched -s read-only -a untrusted, then /plan post-launch, then /status. Timing matters: /plan is REJECTED ('/plan is disabled while a task is in progress') while codex is still booting its MCP servers, so the keystroke waits ~40s. This is the only honest 'plan' session: observed 'plan' on the permissions axis AND collaboration 'Plan'. Compare status-readonly-default, which is byte-identical in launch flags but never ran /plan. Widths are measured from the rendered 120x40 screen. widest_status_row_cols/status_box_outer_cols is the box's rendered width; intrinsic_row_cols is the minimum unwrapped width each row needs (opening bar + label column + value + closing bar), which is what CODEX_STATUS_MIN_COLS has to clear. NOTE the Session: row needs 62 cols, above the current CODEX_STATUS_MIN_COLS = 60 — a documented FOLLOW-UP to raise the constant with this evidence, not a defect here: both the session scrape and the permission parse anchor the closing bar on the same physical line, so a wrapped row fails closed at any width.
